title: Steak Pie

overview.description: Slow-cooked British steak pie with tender beef in a rich gravy, baked beneath golden pastry. Perfect for weekends and excellent for batch cooking.

ingredients: 1kg braising steak, cut into large chunks
250ml ale (optional, replace with extra stock if preferred)
method: === Filling ===
Heat the oil in a large casserole over a medium-high heat.
Brown the beef in batches and set aside.
Cook the onions, carrots and celery until softened, about 10 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the flour and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in the ale, scraping the bottom of the pan.
Add the beef stock, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves and thyme.
Return the beef to the pan.
Cover and cook in a 160°C fan (180°C conventional) oven for 2½–3 hours until the beef is tender.
Remove the bay leaves and allow the filling to cool slightly.
Season to taste.
=== Assemble & Bake ===
Transfer the filling to a pie dish.
Cover with the prepared pastry and trim the edges.
Cut a steam hole in the centre.
Brush with beaten egg.
Bake at 180°C fan (200°C conventional) for 30–35 minutes until deep golden.
Leave to stand for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Steak pie is one of Britain's best-loved comfort foods and is particularly associated with Scotland and northern England.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to braise beef until tender, build a rich gravy and assemble a traditional pie.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A classic British comfort meal that's ideal for batch cooking and freezing. Slow cooking transforms an inexpensive cut of beef into something exceptional.
chapman-tips: Brown the beef in batches to develop maximum flavour.
Allow the filling to cool before adding the pastry.
Chuck steak, braising steak or featherblade all work well.
A splash of ale adds depth but isn't essential.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze either the cooked filling or the assembled pie for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in the oven rather than the microwave to keep the pastry crisp.
